title: Personal Calorie Tracker with Food Photo Upload & Step Tracking

requirements.summary: Build Food Upload screen with photo selection, client-side calorie estimation, and food name suggestion
acceptanceCriteria: User can pick or drag-and-drop a food photo
A food name and calorie estimate are displayed after upload
User can edit the food name and calorie value before confirming
Confirming calls addFoodEntry on the backend and shows a success message
Image preview is shown throughout the flow

file_operations.description: Create Food Upload page component that allows users to select or drag-and-drop a food photo. Display image preview after selection. Use the blob-storage component's ExternalBlob.fromBytes() to prepare the image for backend upload. Implement client-side heuristic calorie estimation based on image filename patterns and basic file size signals (e.g., larger files suggest larger portions). Display suggested food name (derived from filename cleanup) and estimated calorie range. Provide editable input fields for food name and calorie value. On confirm, call backend.addFoodEntry with today's date, the user-confirmed values, and the ExternalBlob image reference. Show success feedback after save. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable drag-and-drop image upload component using shadcn/ui Card and Input components. Support file input click and drag-and-drop events. Display image preview thumbnail once file is selected. Emit selected file to parent component. Include visual feedback for drag-over state and file type validation (accept only image files).

Create client-side heuristic calorie estimation utility. Analyze image filename for common food keywords (e.g., pizza, salad, burger) and map them to typical calorie ranges. Use file size as a secondary signal (larger files may indicate larger portions). Return an object with estimated calories (midpoint of range) and suggested food name (cleaned filename or matched keyword). Keep estimation simple and rule-based without external API calls.
Add route for /upload path pointing to the FoodUpload page component. Ensure navigation is wired so users can access the Food Upload screen from the main navigation menu.

requirements.summary: Build Daily Dashboard screen showing food entries, step input, calorie totals, and net balance with motivational messaging
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard loads today's data by default
Date picker allows viewing any previously logged day
Food entries list shows food name and calories for the selected date
Step count input saves to the backend on submit
Total calories in, calories burned, and net calories are displayed clearly
A calorie deficit is highlighted positively (green); surplus is highlighted as a warning (amber/red)

file_operations.description: Create Daily Dashboard page component that defaults to today's date. Include a date picker (using shadcn/ui Calendar or Input with type=date) to allow selecting any date. On date selection or initial load, call backend.getEntriesForDate and backend.getDailySummary to fetch food entries and daily totals. Display a list of food entries with food name, calorie count, and thumbnail image (use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for each image). Show total calories consumed, total steps, calories burned, and net calorie balance in prominent stat cards. Include an input field for step count that calls backend.getSteps on load and allows user to update via a form that triggers backend method (assuming setSteps capability). Display motivational message based on net calorie sign: green text and positive message for deficit, amber/red warning for surplus. Use shadcn/ui Card, Badge, and Button components for layout and actions.

Create a reusable food entry display component that takes a FoodEntry object. Show the food name, calorie count, and a thumbnail image using the ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() method from the blob-storage component. Use shadcn/ui Card with an image element and text labels. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable stat display component (e.g., total calories in, calories burned, net balance) using shadcn/ui Card. Accept label, value, and optional color variant (success/warning/default). Display large numeric value with smaller label text. Support conditional styling based on variant prop.

Create a custom React hook that fetches daily summary and food entries for a given date. Accept date as parameter and return loading state, error state, food entries array, and summary object (totalCaloriesConsumed, totalSteps, totalCaloriesBurned, netCalories). Use backend.getEntriesForDate and backend.getDailySummary. Implement useEffect to refetch when date changes.
Add route for /dashboard (or set as default root /) pointing to the Dashboard page component. Ensure it is the default landing page or easily accessible via main navigation.

requirements.summary: Build History Log screen listing all logged dates with calorie summaries and navigation to specific date's dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: History screen lists all days that have at least one entry
Each row shows date, calories in, calories burned, net calories
Clicking a date row navigates to the Dashboard for that date
Average daily net calorie figure is shown at the top of the history list

file_operations.description: Create History Log page component that calls backend.getAvailableDates to retrieve all dates with logged entries. For each date, call backend.getDailySummary to get totals (or batch fetch if performance becomes an issue). Display a list of date rows, each showing the date, total calories consumed, total calories burned, and net calories. Make each row clickable (using Link or navigate) to go to /dashboard?date=YYYY-MM-DD. Calculate and display average daily net calories across all logged dates at the top of the list. Use shadcn/ui Table or Card list for layout.

Create a reusable history row component that displays a single date's summary: date label, calories in, calories burned, net calories. Use shadcn/ui TableRow or Card. Support onClick handler to navigate to the Dashboard with that date pre-selected. Include conditional color coding for net calories (green for deficit, red for surplus).

Create a custom React hook that fetches all available dates and their daily summaries. Return an array of objects (date, summary data) and calculate the average net calories across all days. Handle loading and error states.
Add route for /history pointing to the History page component. Ensure it is accessible from the main navigation menu.

requirements.summary: Apply cohesive health-and-wellness visual theme with soft greens, white, warm orange accents, rounded cards, and airy layout
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette (soft greens, white backgrounds, warm orange CTAs) applied throughout
All screens use rounded card components and clear section headings
Navigation between Food Upload, Dashboard, and History is intuitive and always accessible
App feels visually cohesive and polished on both mobile and desktop viewport widths

file_operations.description: Update global CSS variables and base styles to implement the health-and-wellness theme. Define CSS custom properties for soft green primary color, warm orange accent, white background, and appropriate text colors. Set body background to a light neutral or soft green tint. Ensure typography has clear hierarchy with readable font sizes and line heights. Apply border-radius defaults for a rounded, friendly aesthetic.

Extend Tailwind theme with custom color palette matching the design requirement: soft greens (primary), warm orange (accent), white (background), and neutral grays. Configure borderRadius to default to rounded corners (e.g., lg or xl). Ensure spacing scale supports airy layouts. Add any custom utility classes needed for the wellness aesthetic.

Create a Layout component that wraps all pages and provides consistent navigation. Include a header with app logo (use the generated logo-mark.dim_256x256.png from frontend/public/assets/generated/logo-mark.dim_256x256.png) and navigation links to Dashboard, Food Upload, and History. Use shadcn/ui navigation components or custom links styled with the theme. Ensure the layout is responsive and uses the defined color palette. Display the onboarding hero illustration (frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-food.dim_1200x400.png) on the Dashboard page as a hero section to reinforce branding.
Wrap all routes with the Layout component to ensure consistent navigation and branding across all pages. Confirm that routes for Dashboard, Food Upload, and History are all registered and accessible.
